storyInfo: San Sophie, a rusted, ever-progressing city. A retiring detective is wrapping up his last case, only for the uninvited Tin Man to point out certain questionable aspects about the case.

attributes.content: 'Hello. Yes, it's still me. I may not have made it absolutely clear just now. I ordered a single-layer shortcake. Single. On the receipt I got, it says...'
'Single costs seventy gold certificates, too? Okay, fine. No, no need to make it double. Keep Mosby as the recipient. No, not Mosley. Mos... Hold on.'
Alright, I rolled up the window. Can you hear me now? It's Detective MOSBY. Hugh Mosby.
attributes.name: Impatient Man

attributes.content: Please get it to the station by six. Other requests? No, nothing. Just a retirement party. Can't have a party without cake, don't you agree? Okay, good bye.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Have you made up your mind yet? If you have, go ahead and sign your confession.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Why did you drag me out of the interrogation room? I've never heard of cops doing this in a car...
attributes.name: Young Man With Bruised Face

attributes.content: Shut your face hole. I'm asking the questions here. Goddamned murderer.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: I told you, I never even thought about killing him. I was just... defending myself! He's the one who attacked first!
attributes.name: Young Man With Bruised Face

attributes.content: Save your breath. This is the switchblade used to kill the victim, see? And it's covered with your fingerprints. This is what we call evidence. If we were on the frontier, it'd be enough for me to put a bullet in your head.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: I've caught plenty of kids like you. All of you skip school, steal, get all tattooed up, keep talking shit that you don't even know what it means, thinking you are better than everybody else.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: And you know what happened to all of them in the end?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: You'll be no different.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Having lost his patience, the old detective grabs his files and pen and pushes open the door. Immediately, a cold breeze gushes into the car from the harbor.
Wait up! You're retiring, right? This is your last case!
attributes.name: Young Man With Bruised Face

attributes.content: Do you really want your career to end with a false conviction? You must have a really glorious past, huh? Is this really how you want it to end?!
attributes.name: Young Man With Bruised Face

attributes.content: The old detective turns around and glances at the Columbian Police Handbook on the dashboard.
Then, he sets down the confession on the roof of the car and quickly signs it himself.
The impatient breeze folds the pages up for him before the ink can even dry.
Detective Mosby. Not wearing your uniform, huh?
attributes.name: Trainee Police Officer

attributes.content: Morning, Trevor. I handed my uniform back in already. It's my last day anyway. If somebody wants to write me up for this, well, he might as well fire me.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: The suspect signed his confession. Have two of our stronger guys go to the parking lot and drag him into solitary. Then, take this file to the superintendent and get it signed.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Maybe later? The commissioner and the superintendent are in the morning meeting... Alright, fine. I know that's not going to stop you.
attributes.name: Trainee Police Officer

attributes.content: You know I don't want any part in that stuff they're obsessed with. Those bank fundraisers, intercity nets, and whatever.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: All I want to do now is grab the superintendent by his collar and have him sign this off to wrap up my last case. After that, I'll buy my first cup of coffee, and enjoy my last day as a policeman.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Are you gonna have a retirement party today, per tradition?
attributes.name: Trainee Police Officer

attributes.content: Mm-hm. You coming?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Uh... Let's see. Tonight, I still gotta...
attributes.name: Trainee Police Officer

attributes.content: Forget it, then. Haha.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Let go! Let me go! I'm innocent!
attributes.name: Young Man With Bruised Face

attributes.content: Mur... Murderers! I'm innocent, and you're sending an innocent man to his death!
attributes.name: Young Man With Bruised Face

attributes.content: The young man thrashes around, like a wild fin got caught in a net, but all he can do is knock a few documents out of the hands of a passing clerk.
Hey! Watch it! This is for the commissioner's signature!
attributes.name: Unhappy Police Station Clerk

attributes.content: Dammit! All of you, watch where you're going! Don't step on any of them!
attributes.name: Unhappy Police Station Clerk

attributes.content: Slow down, son. This is no big deal. Here in San Sophie, the last thing we'd ever run short of is loan contract printouts.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: The clerk recognizes the man in front of him as the VIP that the commissioner introduced to everyone during the morning meeting. Immediately, he tries to stop the man from bending down to pick up the paperwork.
Please, I can take care of this myself... Mr. Tin Man... or should I call you Mr. Criminology Consultant?
attributes.name: Unhappy Police Station Clerk

attributes.content: Please, call me Tin Man.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: What is it with the young man screaming his lungs out there? Does that happen often here?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: The superintendent's office is still empty? Just how long is he going to keep chumming it up with those bankers and their obnoxious cologne? I need someone to sign my case off!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: The bankers are gone. He should be in a meeting with the guys from the government...
attributes.name: Trainee Police Officer

attributes.content: Another meeting? What's that about?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: The intercity web and whatnot. Nothing you ever cared about.
attributes.name: Trainee Police Officer

attributes.content: Well, I care now! This is going to affect whether I get a retirement party tonight! You can't have cake past midnight. You know that, right?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Real sorry, Detective Mosby, but I have a few questions for you.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: You—Good lord, what the hell is wrong with your body?!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Sorry, an old man like me tends to get spooked at the sight of new things. You're from one of those big cities, right? D.C.? Trimounts? Or Wrankwood?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Something like that. San Sophie Police Special Criminology Consultant, Tin Man. Thank you for your concern. My body is in excellent condition. You can think of the metal as a sort of assistive medical device.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Detective Hugh Mosby. What was the question again? Oh, right. The hotheaded prick? Yeah, that's my case.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: And it's the old man's last case ever. Hey, don't look at me like that. I'm leaving now. Happy retirement, Old Hugh!
attributes.name: Trainee Police Officer

attributes.content: I had a look at the case's records. A typical mugging that turned deadly. The victim is a local, unemployed man.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Hm, looks like you've done your homework. You said you had a question. What is it, 'criminology consultant'?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Please, call me Tin Man. I noticed you're well-versed in cases like these. The chain of evidence you put together is always impeccable, too.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: (Sighs) And your question?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: My question is in regards to the robbery itself, Detective. An unemployed stevedore, thrown out of the bar because he couldn't pay his tab. I'm curious—
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: What exactly is there to rob from a man like him?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: I've handled these hoodlums for decades. I know their type...
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Please, let me finish. My curiosity got the better of me, so I asked my friends in financial crimes to look into this for me. Check here... The victim's bank account was emptied out just the day before he was killed.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Emptied out? Well... Even if that's true, that's a whole separate case.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Yes, but is there really no connection between these two cases? Detective Mosby, I'm afraid you can't go set up your retirement party just yet.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: So I'm supposed to just follow your every word?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Haha, of course not. Naturally, I can't do this without your cooperation. I can't just sign the documents with your signature and pretend you've agreed to everything now, can I?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Please, there is no need to be alarmed. All I want to do is help you out while I get to know this place and this city.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: All the evidence's here. Every single one of them has been labeled and categorized. Look all you want.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Is the label printer broken?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: What the hell is a label printer?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Never mind. Why isn't the victim's financial details included in his background check?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: His bank account is with one of those... what do you call them... Those intercity net virtual banks. They don't have any branches, so I couldn't get in touch with them.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Not even the IT department could help you?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: I didn't bother asking them. They wouldn't care even if I did.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: This is too small of a case. You get dozens of these every month. Don't think it's a problem, if you asked me.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Dozens a month? This is a huge issue, Detective.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Is this terminal the victim's?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Yeah. I hauled it all the way here from his place. Pretty darn heavy.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: To bring in new customers, that bank didn't just open bank accounts for free. They even handed out these terminals for connecting to the intercity net. No idea what's in this for them, really. Government subsidies?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: No need to be so cynical. San Sophie's embrace of the intercity net and its many financial precautions have made it the leading example of city reinvigoration in Columbia.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Cynical? Haha. They have workers fork over their hard-earned life savings to some manager they've never met. Just the perfect effing example for a civilized society.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Have you tried turning on this terminal?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: No.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: There should be some relevant transaction records stored on it... Hold on, where is its storage module? The CPU isn't here, either?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Detective Mosby... You didn't bring back only the monitor, did you?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: (Shuts eyes) Damn it—
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Wait for me here. I need to buy myself a goddamn cup of coffee to scrape together enough sanity for this last goddamned case. You want one?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Thanks, but no need. I have a few phone calls to make.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: What a freaking headache. I need at least a double shot.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: The detective turns the corner to the traditional, pioneer-style coffee shop that he has patronized for decades.
He has already come up with a few jokes for the barista, who'd burst out laughing even at the worst ones, every single time. Yesterday, he almost spilled coffee grounds all over the floor.
He stops in his tracks. The shop in front of him is not the familiar coffee shop he visited just yesterday.
What the crap is this? Why is there only a freaking kiosk here? Where did the coffee shop go?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Hey, what gives?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Not a single passerby stops to answer his question, leaving him standing there to be deafened by the noise of the bank's flag repeatedly slapping the construction warning sign.

attributes.name: Tin Man
attributes.content: Please slow down a little. This is not a race—

attributes.name: Tin Man
attributes.content: Hello! Yes, I just called you earlier. It's still about that case. The victim's name is—
(Covers phone) What's his name again?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Granger.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Joseph Granger. I need all of his account's transaction records for the past month... You can't give them to me? My apologies. Perhaps I'm not making myself clear enough...
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Please look for the copy of San Sophie City Intercity Net Establishment Act that has likely been sitting on your desk untouched since it was distributed two days ago. Read the very first line at the very top.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: This document was signed by the mayor himself. And please don't tell me you don't have it, Mr. Bank Manager. Otherwise, either you or the mayor... or both might have to look forward to a court date.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: That's right. 'Under the supervision of the city government and all its citizens.' Now, take a look at the second page regarding the repercussions for refusal to cooperate with law enforcement.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Haha, of course I have that authority. My name? Call me Tin Man.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Smart guy. Yes, I am listening. Bankruptcy? His savings were transferred in one single transaction? Is the recipient a business account? Personal. Okay.
attributes.name: Tin Man
Tin Man
attributes.content: A mortgage? How much?

attributes.name: Tin Man
attributes.content: I am surprised you approved a loan to that amount so quickly. Whew, how shockingly efficient.
I will need you to fax all the documents to the San Sophie Police. Detective Hugh Mosby for the recipient. Good bye.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Hey...
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: The communicator in the Tin Man's hand rings once again to interrupt the detective.
Yes, hello. Mr. Nixon of the Nixon Cargo Handling Company, am I correct? Thank you for calling back. I just wanted to make sure. Was there a Joseph Granger under your employ?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Yes, that's right. There will be an investigation into the legality of your company's layoffs, but I actually want to know a little more about Mr. Granger's workman's compensation insurance.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Accident insurance? To the amount of... 1.7 million gold certificates. Very good. Is he still being compensated? He hasn't been paid for that since he left? Did he know about it?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: You should've told him much sooner. Rest assured, I'll be in touch, Mr. Nixon.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Almost as soon as the Tin Man hangs up, the detective starts to speak.
Heh, you're not so bad at this.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: I'm good enough. I'm actually surprised that these basic communication techniques can impress an old veteran like you.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: You think you're so damn cool chewing them out on the communicator, huh?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: 'Of course I have that authority,' 'Rest assured, I'll be in touch...' What, you think you're in a Wrankwood courtroom flick?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Tell you the truth, I actually considered starring in one of those.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: So that's how you figured everything out just by making a couple phone calls. Bravo!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: A bankrupt, unemployed man, provoking a hoodlum on purpose to try to make enough money off his accident insurance—one that expired long ago—to pay off his debt. Is that the story you're going to tell the brass?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Detective Mosby slams the horn, then suddenly turns, before switching lanes to overtake a slow truck.
Easy, Detective. Drive safe.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: You know what this city used to look like forty years ago? It was nothing but a swath of barrenlands! It was us pioneers who carried each rock here one-by-one to build this city from the ground up!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: You young'uns from the big cities with all your jargon and those communicators. You think you can crack every case just by calling every goddamn number out there on Terra and blabbering into the phone!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: You know what I had when I first became a cop? I had the shirt on my back, and this shitty little police handbook!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: The detective grabs the Columbian Police Handbook sitting on his dashboard and tosses it toward the backseat.
It tells us why murders murder and why burglars burglarize, but it doesn't talk about any goddamn intercity net, bank accounts, or frickin' insurance agents!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: The times are changing, Detective. I don't understand where your anger is coming from.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: You don't understand? I don't understand, either!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: It's because of you dipshits chasing trends and grabbing the newest gizmos! You people are why our perfectly fine city turned into this shitheap! To the point that a simple coffee shop couldn't stay in business!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Listen up, Tin Fucking Man. This is my case and nobody else's. I've been a cop for thirty years, and I don't need a steel-plated guy from a movie to come teach me how to solve it!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Someone's communicator starts ringing. Immediately, the detective glares at the Tin Man, leaving him no choice but to lift his device up to show that he hasn't received any call.
With a grumpy look, Detective Mosby pulls out his own communicator and answers the call.

Hello, Mr. Mosley. I'm very sorry to tell you that I've made a mistake with your order. There are actually seven other customers ahead of you today, so we may not be able to finish your cake in time...
attributes.name: Cake Shop Apprentice

attributes.content: We can give you a full refund! And a couple gift coupons...
attributes.name: Cake Shop Apprentice

attributes.content: I don't need your dogshit coupons! I ordered a freakin' cake! I want a freakin' cake! For my retirement party!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Are you saying I should cancel my party?! Is that what you're telling me?!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: You want me to walk into the frigging office tomorrow with my head hung low, so the greenhorns can laugh at me?!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: I-I'm sorry...
attributes.name: Cake Shop Apprentice

attributes.content: The detective hangs up.
Tin Man rolls down the window and lights a cigarette.
He looks outside. What was once a bustling port is now deserted. Over at the docks, a massive, abandoned cargo landship is being taken apart and sold for scrap.
Here we are. Remember, this is my case. I don't need your help.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: A group of workers with metal tools have surrounded the shabby two-story attic. Inside, a girl hides behind the curtains, trembling from head to toe.
Do you know the people who live here?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: We're just doing the job we're paid to do, boss.
attributes.name: Hired Hand

attributes.content: Here, have a smoke. Tell me, man, why are you making things so hard for this young lady here?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Please don't make this hard for us, boss. This house's been foreclosed, and we're just here to clean up the place. The money comes down from on high, and we depend on the money to live, that's all.
attributes.name: Hired Hand

attributes.content: Who hired you?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: We have no clue either. We just go where the job tells us to go, and once everything's taken care of, they transfer the money to our accounts. If we don't finish, we'll be six feet under.
attributes.name: Hired Hand

attributes.content: The workers take the cigarettes, but no one starts to smoke. The hammers, shovels, and crowbars in their hands are all printed with the stevedores' union's logo.
(Sighs) So you boys used to be dockworkers? The owner of this place was a guy called Joseph Granger. He used to work at the dock too.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: People die, boss. It ain't easy to survive.
attributes.name: Hired Hand

attributes.content: I'm not your boss. Here's my badge.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Per the Columbian Police Handbook, I hereby announce this property was involved in criminal activity and is being sealed off by the San Sophie Police. This whole area is off limits.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: It's no use, boss. We leave, and they'll just send more guys over.
attributes.name: Hired Hand

attributes.content: Scram! Now!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Without protesting, the workers all turn around expressionless and leave.
That's not in the handbook.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: What, you've read every single clause?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: The detective sneers, and picks up the monitor from the trunk of the car.
He forces his way past the Tin Man and walks straight into the house.
It's heavy, isn't it?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: I told you. I don't need—
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: I am just asking.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: The Tin Man walks toward the soggy-looking plywood cabinet, and finds the lost storage module among the pile of household goods that CSI rummaged through.
Here.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: The old detective crouches in front of a pile of electronic components, contemplates for a while, before hesitantly grabbing what seems to be a power cord to plug into the monitor.
The Tin Man clears his throat. The old detective immediately stops what he's doing, turns around, and glares.
I've said not a thing.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: The old detective turns his attention back to the components and frowns. After a brief moment, as though he has figured out something, he grabs one of he cables and connects two crystal chips with it.
Then, seemingly getting the hang of it, he quickly puts together most of the outer shell of the storage module.
But just as he's about to connect the storage module to the monitor, he notices that the two-prong plug in his hand cannot be plugged into the three-hole socket.
Gah... shit!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Maybe it's because he's been crouching for quite some time, but his face turns red, and drops of sweat start to drip down from his forehead.
Despite the plug being obviously incompatible for the socket, he uses his entire strength to press it down.
But, under the intense force, the metal prongs slip and the rugged outer shell slides down, leaving a long, bloody cut on the detective's finger.
Son of a bitch!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Damn your coffee shop! Damn your kiosks!
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: The old detective angrily swings his injured finger around, lashing out at the cup of espresso that he was unable to have for the first time in decades, and the neighbors who post warning letters on his apartment's door.
He lashes out at the politicians who ordered the scrapping of the cargo landships as well as the police commissioner who's willing to turn a blind eye when bribed.
He lashes out at the loopholes in the law, and the coward who left his daughter behind.
He lashes out at the mastermind who rounded up the workers and sent them here. He lashes out at how they are hiding behind the transactions through the intercity net.
He lashes out at his fists for being unable to reach anything at all.
The Tin Man stands idly by. Per the old detective's request, he offers no help until the detective has calmed down.
Put this thing back together, will you? I'm begging you.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: The old detective sits listlessly on the floor.
Tin Man steps over his leg and picks up a small handbook from the floor. It is the terminal's assembly instructions. It has actually been in plain view the entire time.
The instructions come with simple illustrations that are easy to understand. The Tin Man flips through the book page by page, and in no time, he has finished putting the terminal back together.
It's locked behind a password.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: His daughter's birthday.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: The detective says a string of numbers, and a pleasant boot-up sound effect can be heard. The Tin Man has opened the only program on the terminal—an online banking software.
...This is all a scam.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: The scammers lured Granger in with a small amount that could buy him a pack of beer. Then, they started forcing him send more and more money.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: The Tin Man downloads all the conversations and transaction records.
A scam... This kind of scam has never been seen before in San Sophie.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: At the current stage, you could even say this is all legal. These records are practically the same as normal investments.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: But from the very beginning, their goal had always been to bleed Granger dry.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: They even sent him the details on how he could take out a loan with his house as collateral. Wait... they also mentioned... accident insurance.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Okay, that's more than enough. That's abetting suicide. They aren't getting away.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: No, this is far from enough. You have no way of finding out who is behind this. It's very likely that it's not just one person on the other side.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: They didn't leave him so much as a single gold certificate.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: When did people start acting like this? Back on the frontier, not even the worst of thieves would steal the last piece of bread from a poor family. What happened to our humanity?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Back then, everyone still dueled in broad daylight... Come, let's head back to the station. We have all the evidence we needed.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: The Tin Man puts the evidence into his pocket and leaves the room.
The old detective, however, is still staring at the monitor that has been off for some time. The man reflected on the screen seems unrecognizable.
By the time he's finally made it out of the building, he finds the Tin Man discussing something on his communicator again. A surge of rage out of nowhere starts to boil within the old detective.
...Okay, it's settled, then. Goodbye.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: And what are you doing now?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: I've contacted the Maylander Child Rights Protection Foundation. Their specialists will be here soon to find Granger's daughter a suitable place to live.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Is this not the humanity that you wanted, Detective?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: This is supposed to be my job, and I didn't do my job.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: You did the right thing.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: The old detective laughs at himself mockingly, as though a weight has been lifted off his shoulders.
I'm too old, alright. I'm not cut out for this job anymore.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: The old detective's communicator rings.
Mr. Mosley, I'm so sorry for the experience you had earlier. Our manager has reprimanded me, and I've learned my lesson.
attributes.name: Cake Shop Apprentice

attributes.content: We made some adjustments on our end. We'll have your retirement cake delivered to you at exactly the time you requested.
attributes.name: Cake Shop Apprentice

attributes.content: No, son, I'm the one who owes you an apology. I shouldn't have acted like that. I don't need the cake anymore... No, I don't need a refund. What I'm saying is, leave this chance for somebody who needs it more.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Party? Forget it, son. No one was gonna come anyway.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: You should head back to the station. I'll wait here for my coworkers to come pick the girl up.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: The old detective bandages his injured finger and squeezes himself into the car. Just then, the Tin Man calls out to him.
You made that regulation up, didn't you?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Ha. Believe it or not, all these years, whenever I come across something that I don't want to deal with or want no part in, I make up some kind of regulation and say it's in the handbook.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: It's been so many years, and you're the first one to call me on it.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: At the very least, you've actually studied the handbook.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: What else would I study? The Lateran folks got their prayers and hymns, while all I have is an old book. A rookie like you wouldn't understand.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: ...Goodbye.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Later that night at the police station, the old detective keeps rewriting the report over and over.
Is the party over?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Yeah, you missed the cake.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: The old detective rolls his eyes, and takes a cup of hot coffee from the Tin Man's hand.
I'll pay you back tomorrow.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: You let that hooligan go the moment you got back to the station. Are you still coming in for work tomorrow?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: I still need to turn in my badge. Haven't had the time to see the commissioner all day.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Perhaps I can hand it in for you? In a few hours, I'll be locking them in the conference room for some time.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: No... There are a few things I need to tell him myself.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: The Tin Man nods, then pulls out a thick book and hands it over to the old detective.
Here's a retirement gift for you. It hasn't been published yet, so keep it to yourself.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Columbian Police Handbook... Second Edition?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Third, actually. The one you've been reading is the second. When I wrote that one, San Sophie was indeed a completely different place.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: You... You?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Is that so surprising? You're not the only old man who walks these lands.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: My original plan was to stay in the city for three days, and after a few rounds of important meetings, proceed to other cities to conduct on-site observations there.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: But thanks to your case today, I'm planning to stay here for a few more days.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Observe what? Cops?
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Policemen, criminals, the unemployed, power brokers, broken electric fans... I'll observe everything that urgently awaits change. I'll observe and learn from it.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Alright.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: You accepted that quickly. Why?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: All I've accepted is the reality that I'm old. Columbia these days, you wake up to all kinds of new things every morning. I'm getting used to it.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: If I can't bring myself to accept the changes the city is going through, I can still move to the frontier and live my life the way I want.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: I'll bake cookies for my neighbors, chit-chat with the barkeep, instead of worrying about having my life ruined by some strangers.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: And even the bandits there would at least leave me a bloody handprint at the crime scenes.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: Every second I lived is pulling me back. I don't have the strength to change myself anymore.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: No one will blame a man for wanting to stay in the past, for being a pioneer of his own life.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: That said, someone has got to look at what's ahead, isn't that right?
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: I am the poor man who must keep chasing tomorrow, as the place I must go is much farther away than tomorrow.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Thanks for talking all this over with me, Tin Man. It's a good thing you screwed up my last case today.
attributes.name: Detective Mosby

attributes.content: And it's a good thing that I gave myself half a day of free time before my meetings. Otherwise, I would never have met the detective with the worst driving skills I've ever seen, witnessed the most realistic incident in this country, or revealed this well-hidden corner to the public.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: I believe my work will unmask those who are currently evading justice. And to get there, I need the help of many more people like you.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: Don't worry about tomorrow, Detective. Tomorrow always comes. Happy retirement.
attributes.name: Tin Man

attributes.content: The detective holds the unpublished book in one hand and the cup of coffee in the other, as though he is saluting someone.
Until the first ray of light spills through the window blinds and bathes him in a soft glow.
